description: Fed into transcript_sense_scan LLM system prompt. Edit to match your ledger / house style.
series: Bride of Charlie (investigative podcast, YouTube captions)

family_core.notes: Maiden / bio surname; on camera often Erika Kirk after marriage.

institutions_and_programs.notes: Paradise Valley area school; STT varies. Project house spelling: Tesseract. Investigation node name should use this canonical form.
Historical CIA program; always emit an InvestigationTarget node when substantively discussed.
When the episode refers to the CIA program by this name, use a dedicated InvestigationTarget; do not subsume under a school node.
President of France; STT often mangles both names into one blob.

stt_often: France (when clearly a person name, not the country)

surname_stt.notes: Intentional Candace pun ep5 (~1:31); not a real psychologist.

instructions_for_model: Flag 'Tesaract' when it refers to the school and suggested_fix should use 'Tesseract' per institutions_and_programs.
If the transcript uses an stt_often form in a name context, flag it (usually high severity) and suggested_fix should use the canonical spelling.
Do NOT flag correct canonical spellings as errors.
Country 'France' in geopolitical context is fine; 'France' as a stand-in for Frantzve in a name list is an error.
Rhetoric and opinions are not errors.

When the transcript mentions the president of France, suing, or Tim Dillon in the same breath as a mangled name, treat obvious STT blobs (e.g. Mcronone) as errors and map to Emmanuel Macron per glossary.
